2605.08585.title: PromptDx: Differentiable Prompt Tuning for Multimodal In-Context Alzheimer's Diagnosis
2605.08585.paper_summary: Deep learning models in medical imaging typically operate as parametric memory, diagnosing patients by recalling fixed knowledge learned during training. This contrasts sharply with clinical practice, where physicians employ analogical reasoning to diagnose new cases by referencing similar records from past exemplars. While In-Context Learning (ICL) frameworks such as Tabular Prior-Fitted Networks (TabPFN) offer a promising diagnosis-by-reference paradigm, they are designed with tabular-specific inductive priors and rely on non-differentiable preprocessing pipelines, leading to manifold mismatch and gradient fracture when applied to heterogeneous multimodal data. To address these limitations, we propose PromptDx, a novel diagnosis-by-reference framework that leverages a pre-trained TabPFN as an ICL engine while enabling seamless integration with multimodal representations. Our core contribution is a Differentiable Prompt Tuning (DPT) mechanism that aligns a Masked Multimodal Modeling module with the pre-trained ICL engine. By training a lightweight adapter as a differentiable surrogate for the engine's non-differentiable preprocessors, we enable an end-to-end optimization of multimodal prompts within the ICL paradigm. We validate our method on the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) dataset using 3D MRI and tabular biomarkers. Experiments demonstrate that our approach outperforms traditional parametric baselines. Notably, our method achieves superior performance using only 1% context samples compared to 30% in standard ICL, demonstrating exceptional manifold condensation ability. We further validate the generalizability of our DPT framework across six tabular datasets with diverse scales. Overall, our method offers a more data-efficient and clinically aligned paradigm for Alzheimer's Disease diagnosis.
2605.08585.paper_summary_zh: 深度學習模型在醫學影像中通常作為參數化記憶運作，通過回憶訓練期間學習的固定知識來診斷患者。這與臨床實踐形成鮮明對比，醫生通過參考過去範例的相似記錄來進行類比推理以診斷新病例。儘管像表格先驗擬合網絡（TabPFN）這樣的上下文學習（ICL）框架提供了一種有前景的參考診斷範式，但它們是以特定於表格的歸納先驗設計的，並依賴於不可微分的預處理管道，這在應用於異質多模態數據時會導致流形不匹配和梯度斷裂。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了PromptDx，一種新穎的參考診斷框架，利用預訓練的TabPFN作為ICL引擎，同時實現與多模態表示的無縫集成。我們的核心貢獻是一種可微調提示（DPT）機制，將一個掩蔽多模態建模模塊與預訓練的ICL引擎對齊。通過訓練一個輕量級適配器作為引擎不可微分預處理器的可微分替代，我們實現了在ICL範式中對多模態提示的端到端優化。我們在阿茲海默病神經影像倡議（ADNI）數據集上使用3D MRI和表格生物標記驗證我們的方法。實驗表明，我們的方法超越了傳統的參數基準。值得注意的是，我們的方法在僅使用1%的上下文樣本時達到了優越的性能，而標準ICL則需要30%，顯示出卓越的流形凝聚能力。我們進一步驗證了我們的DPT框架在六個具有不同規模的表格數據集上的可泛化性。總體而言，我們的方法為阿茲海默病診斷提供了一種更具數據效率和臨床對齊的範式。
2605.08585.author: Lujia Zhong et.al.
2605.08585.authors: Lujia Zhong, Yihao Xia, Shuo Huang, Jianwei Zhang, Yonggang Shi
